chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَلْزَمُ مَنْ يَحْفِرُ الْبِئْرَ فَيَقَعُ فِيهَا الْمَارُّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – What necessitates the one who digs the well, so there falls in it the passer-by

text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مَنْ أَخْرَجَ مِيزَاباً أَوْ كَنِيفاً أَوْ أَوْتَدَ وَتِداً أَوْ أَوْثَقَ دَابَّةً أَوْ حَفَرَ بِئْراً فِي طَرِيقِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَأَصَابَ شَيْئاً فَعَطِبَ فَهُوَ لَهُ ضَامِنٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The one who extracts a water spout, or a toilet (enclosure), or fixes a peg, or fastens an animal, or digs a well in the road of the Muslims, so something is damaged, so he would be responsible for it’.
